display.purpose: Start-state prompts and empty screen questions.

title.purpose: Composer text, transcript body emphasis, and compact content titles.

body.purpose: Navigation rows, buttons, transcript metadata, project copy, and normal UI text.

label.purpose: Badges, local menu items, and compact control labels.

caption.purpose: Status captions, error metadata, and helper text.

shadows.name: context-menu-float
shadows.purpose: Sidebar and context menus above dense navigation.

motion.name: context-motion
motion.purpose: Explain state changes, scope shifts, progressive reveals, and active runtime status.

motion.name: sidebar-transition
motion.purpose: Preserve spatial context when the sidebar collapses or expands.

motion.name: section-reveal
motion.purpose: Show nested chat hierarchy opening or closing.

motion.name: quick-affordance
motion.purpose: Reveal hover-only icons and action affordances without distracting from the task.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Shorten or remove nonessential movement while preserving the final state change.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Compact primary product control for direct actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border: 1px solid var(--color-border); border-radius: var(--radius-control); background: var(--color-primary); color: var(--color-primary-foreground); font: 500 var(--type-body)/1.2 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background-color 180ms ease, color 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--color-primary) 90%, var(--color-background)); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--color-ring) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Ghost Control
components.description: Quiet model, scope, or toolbar control that reveals affordance on hover.
components.css: .ds-ghost-control { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.375rem; border: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-control); background: transparent; color: var(--color-muted-foreground); font: 400 var(--type-body)/1.2 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background-color 160ms ease, color 160ms ease; } .ds-ghost-control:hover, .ds-ghost-control[aria-expanded='true'] { background: var(--color-accent); color: var(--color-accent-foreground); } .ds-ghost-control:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Composer Surface
components.description: Raised active writing surface for starting or steering a Pi session.
components.css: .ds-composer { width: min(100%, 46rem); min-height: 96px; display: grid; gap: 0.75rem; border-radius: var(--radius-composer, 1.5rem); background: #2d2d2d; padding: var(--composer-padding, 0.75rem) var(--composer-padding, 0.75rem) 0.25rem; box-shadow: 0 0.875rem 1.375rem -0.875rem rgb(0 0 0 / 30%); color: var(--color-foreground); } .ds-composer-input { width: 100%; min-height: 2.75rem; resize: none; border: 0; background: transparent; color: var(--color-foreground); font: 400 var(--type-composer)/1.4 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; outline: none; } .ds-composer-input::placeholder { color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--color-muted-foreground) 78%, transparent); } .ds-composer-input:focus-visible { border-radius: var(--radius-control); outline: 2px solid var(--color-ring); outline-offset: 0.25rem; } .ds-composer-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; } .ds-ghost-control { border: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-control); background: transparent; color: var(--color-muted-foreground); padding: 0.25rem 0.375rem; font: 400 var(--type-body)/1.2 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-ghost-control:hover { background: var(--color-accent); color: var(--color-accent-foreground); } .ds-send { margin-left: auto; width: 2rem; height: 2rem; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 999px; background: var(--color-primary); color: var(--color-primary-foreground); font: 600 var(--type-body)/1 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-send:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Sidebar Active Row
components.description: Selected project or chat row in the persistent left rail.
components.css: .ds-sidebar-row { width: 100%; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; border: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-control); background: var(--color-secondary); color: var(--sidebar-chat-color); padding: 0.5rem; font: 400 var(--type-body)/1.2 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; text-align: left; transition: background-color 140ms ease, color 140ms ease; } .ds-sidebar-row:hover { background: var(--color-accent); color: var(--color-accent-foreground); } .ds-sidebar-row:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-ring); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-sidebar-icon { width: 1rem; height: 1rem; color: currentColor; } .ds-sidebar-label { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Transcript Work Card
components.description: Inspectable file or tool-effect record inside a coding transcript.
components.html:
□
Edited 6 files
+168 -29
components.css: .ds-work-card { display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto minmax(0, 1fr) auto; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; min-height: 4.5rem; border: 1px solid var(--color-border); border-radius: var(--radius-panel); background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--color-card) 86%, transparent); padding: 0.75rem; color: var(--color-foreground); } .ds-work-card-icon { display: inline-flex; width: 2.5rem; height: 2.5rem; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border-radius: var(--radius-panel); background: var(--color-background); color: var(--color-muted-foreground); } .ds-work-card-copy { min-width: 0; } .ds-work-card-title { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; font: 400 var(--type-composer)/1.3 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-work-card-subtitle { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; color: var(--color-muted-foreground); font: 400 var(--type-body)/1.3 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-work-card-action { border: 1px solid var(--color-border); border-radius: var(--radius-control); background: transparent; color: var(--color-foreground); padding: 0.375rem 0.625rem; font: 400 var(--type-body)/1.2 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-work-card-action:hover { background: var(--color-accent); color: var(--color-accent-foreground); } .ds-work-card-action:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Floating Menu
components.description: Compact contextual command surface for project, chat, and composer controls.
components.css: .ds-menu { min-width: 13rem; border: 1px solid var(--color-border); border-radius: var(--menu-popover-radius); background: var(--menu-popover-background); padding: var(--menu-popover-padding); color: var(--menu-popover-color); box-shadow: 0 12px 32px oklch(0 0 0 / 35%); } .ds-menu-item { width: 100%; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--menu-item-gap); border: 0; border-radius: var(--menu-item-radius); background: transparent; color: inherit; padding: var(--menu-item-padding); font: 400 var(--type-body)/1.2 Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; text-align: left; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-menu-item:hover { background: var(--menu-item-hover-background); color: var(--color-accent-foreground); } .ds-menu-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-ring); outline-offset: 1px; } .ds-menu-item-danger { color: var(--sidebar-warning-color); }

narrative.overview:
Pi Desktop lives in the new desktop coding-agent category, where users already expect a left session rail, a centered start composer, a compact runtime header, and inspectable transcript output. The system should honor that emerging familiarity while staying original, open-source, and tied to Pi's local runtime model.

The visual language is a Graphite Workbench: dark tonal surfaces, compact macOS density, scarce accent color, and controls that stay quiet until state matters. A developer should trust the interface as a daily work surface, not a spectacle.

Motion is part of the workbench. It should preserve spatial context, clarify what changed, reveal hierarchy, and mark active runtime status. It should stay short, interruptible, and reduced-motion-safe.

The system explicitly rejects chatbot toy feel, SaaS dashboard clichés, neon hacker styling, hidden autonomy, and decorative motion. It should feel local, precise, and inspectable, with enough category familiarity that users can transfer habits from current desktop coding-agent tools.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, low-chroma graphite neutrals with one scarce blue state accent and a clear red danger family.
Compact controls, small radii, and macOS-oriented side navigation.
Tonal layering first, shadows only for raised composer and floating menus.
Motion that explains state, scope, reveal, and runtime changes without animating layout properties.
Transcript and file-effect surfaces that make agent work inspectable.
Visible scope, status, and recovery context wherever the user can act.

rules.name: The Scarce Ink Rule
rules.body: Blue and red must communicate state, risk, or runtime status. They are forbidden as decoration.

rules.name: The Graphite Step Rule
rules.body: Separate surfaces by adjacent tonal steps before adding borders or shadows.

rules.name: The No Pure Black Rule
rules.body: Avoid pure black or pure white in new tokens. Neutrals should stay slightly lifted from extremes, even when very dark.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Use one sans family unless a dedicated code viewer or terminal surface requires mono text.

rules.name: The Density Rule
rules.body: Product text should stay compact and scannable. Do not introduce oversized marketing type into task surfaces.

rules.name: The Tonal-First Rule
rules.body: Add a tonal step before adding a shadow.

rules.name: The Floating-Only Rule
rules.body: Shadows belong on composer and menus. Static panels, rows, and transcript cards stay flat.

rules.name: The Context Motion Rule
rules.body: Motion should explain state changes, scope shifts, progressive reveals, and active runtime status. Use transform, opacity, clip, and color instead of layout properties.

dos: Do keep workspace scope, active path, session status, and failure context visible around user actions.
Do use graphite tonal steps before adding borders or shadows.
Do reserve State Attention Blue for status, toggles, selected state, retry, and future information states.
Do keep controls compact, familiar, and keyboard reachable.
Do use transcript cards to show tool, file, terminal, and diff effects as inspectable records.
Do use motion to explain state changes, reveal hierarchy, and preserve spatial context.
Do follow the emerging desktop coding-agent layout pattern: persistent left rail, central composer, scoped session header, and inspectable transcript.
donts: Don't create a chatbot toy feel with playful assistant chrome, novelty avatars, or casual agent framing.
Don't use SaaS dashboard clichés: generic metric cards, marketing gradients, repeated icon-card grids, or management-console decoration.
Don't use neon hacker styling, terminal cosplay, novelty cyber visuals, or saturated inactive states.
Don't hide autonomy. Tool execution, workspace scope, filesystem effects, runtime failures, and active session state must stay visible.
Don't animate layout properties such as width, height, grid tracks, margin, or padding. Use transform, opacity, clip, and color instead.
Don't use decorative motion, bounce, elastic easing, indefinite attention loops, or motion that hides latency.
Don't copy Codex app pixels, proprietary assets, or implementation details. Use the category pattern as a reference and keep Pi Desktop original.
Don't use side-stripe borders, gradient text, default glassmorphism, hero metrics, or identical card grids.
